title.en: HANDLING OF PARTICLES OR IONISING RADIATION NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR; IRRADIATION DEVICES; GAMMA RAY OR X-RAY MICROSCOPES
Arrangements for handling particles or ionising radiation, e.g. focusing or moderating
using diaphragms, collimators
using diffraction, refraction or reflection, e.g. monochromators (G21K1/10, G21K7/00 take precedence)

Deviation, concentration or focusing of the beam by electric or magnetic means (electron-optical arrangements in electric discharge tubes H01J29/46; {details, e.g. electric or magnetic deviating means for direct voltage accelerators or in accelerators using single pulses H05H5/02; arrangements for injecting particles into orbits H05H7/08; arrangements for ejecting particles from orbits H05H7/10})
Scattering devices; Absorbing devices; Ionising radiation filters
using charge exchange devices, e.g. for neutralising or changing the sign of the electrical charges of beams (producing or accelerating neutral particle beams H05H3/00)
using polarising devices, e.g. for obtaining a polarised beam {(ion sources, ion guns H01J27/02; polarised targets for producing nuclear reactions H05H6/005)}
for confining charged particles or handling confined charged particles, e.g. ion traps
for confining neutral particles or handling confined neutral particles, e.g. atom traps

Conversion screens for the conversion of the spatial distribution of X-rays or particle radiation into visible images, e.g. fluoroscopic screens (photographic processes using X-ray intensifiers G03C5/17; discharge tubes comprising luminescent screens H01J1/62; cathode ray tubes for X-ray conversion with optical output H01J31/50)
{characterised by the external panel structure}
{with an intermediate layer}
{with a phosphor layer}
{with a binder in the phosphor layer}
{with a protective film}
{with a support}
Irradiation devices (discharge tubes for irradiating H01J37/00)
having no beam-forming means
with beam-forming means
Holders for targets or for other objects to be irradiated
with provision for relative movement of beam source and object to be irradiated
Gamma- or X-ray microscopes
Arrangements for handling radiation or particles
using diffractive, refractive or reflecting elements
Particular details of imaging devices or methods using ionizing electromagnetic radiation such as X-rays or gamma rays
Methods and devices obtaining contrast from non-absorbing interaction of the radiation with matter, e.g. phase contrast
